criteria.good: Parts are integrated only when service access, inspection, and interface clarity remain visible.
criteria.bad: Monolithic body hides removable modules, datums, or inspection paths.
criteria.loop_feedback: Consolidate only low-risk covers or labels; keep service modules, datums, and inspection windows visible.

criteria.good: Fastener patterns, pin joints, slots, bosses, and mating clearances are explicitly represented.
criteria.bad: Joints are described in prose but not represented as holes, bosses, pins, slots, or datums.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add visible fastener holes, bosses, joint pins, and clearance notes for each critical interface.

criteria.good: A reviewer can see what to inspect and why without opening raw JSON.
criteria.bad: Design has no callouts, access windows, labels, datum pads, or verification focus.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add inspection callouts, datum labels, visible windows, and verification checks tied to geometry features.

criteria.good: Critical joints state locking method (locknut, insert, washer, adhesive note) and torque/preload intent; bolt patterns match load direction.
criteria.bad: Fasteners appear as bare holes with no locking, washer, engagement-length, or preload language anywhere.
criteria.loop_feedback: For each critical joint, name the locking feature, add washer/insert geometry, and state a torque or preload intent note.

criteria.good: Threaded connections into printed or soft material use inserts or bosses with adequate engagement depth (~1.5x diameter) and wall around the hole.
criteria.bad: Screws thread directly into thin printed walls or blind features with no boss, insert, or depth note.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add insert bosses with engagement depth >= 1.5x screw diameter and surrounding wall thickness for every threaded joint.
